name: Frohwerk v. United States
question: Did Frohwerk's conviction under the Espionage Act of 1917 violate his right to free speech under the First Amendment?
description: A case in which the Court held that the conviction of a defendant for publishing articles criticizing the US involvement in World War I under the Espionage Act of 1917 does not violate the publisher's free speech rights under the First Amendment.
